projects.name: Padstow Estuary Monitoring System
projects.description: A consolidated FutureScaping workspace for repeated estuary surveys, combining project context, imagery outputs, section profiles, and operational notes in one reusable interface.

site.name: Padstow Harbour / Camel Estuary
site.context: The original workflow proved the value of the survey outputs, but the apps are currently duplicated and Padstow-specific. This version starts turning that into one reusable monitoring system.

programme.operationalLesson: The 3DVista-linked workflow works as a prototype, but repeated surveys become difficult to manage when each output lives in a separate hard-coded app.
programme.consolidationGoal: Add future survey rounds as data records and asset paths, rather than cloning more standalone apps.

surveyAssetModel.note: The baseline survey round has now been copied into the new survey-data structure. Later rounds still need their own processed outputs copied into matching survey folders.

legacyApps.name: Comparison App
legacyApps.description: Ortho, DSM, and contour layers by area.

legacyApps.name: Slider App
legacyApps.description: Image comparison pattern for repeat surveys.

legacyApps.name: Padstow Dashboard
legacyApps.description: Weather, tides, and survey timing context.

surveys.label: Survey Round 1 - 22 and 23 March 2026
surveys.notes: First completed survey round and current baseline in the monitoring system.

area8.tideWindow: Falling tide well ahead of low water
area8.missionRole: Opened Day 1 of the April repeat survey and covered the upper Wadebridge end before low tide.
area8.operationalNote: This block used the first two flights of the day, giving a long early-tide window before the estuary reached low water.
area8.weatherNotes: Day 1, 18 April 2026. Early survey window with the tide still falling and plenty of water remaining across the upper estuary. Low tide reference for Wadebridge / upper estuary was about 12:56 BST.
area8.surveyNotes: This was the first area covered on the 18 April scan day, using the first two flights of the day. It gives an early-tide picture of the upper estuary before the system moved down-estuary towards the lower water windows.
area8.purpose: Shows the Wadebridge end of the estuary well before low water, giving context for how much water was still present at the start of the April repeat survey.

area7.tideWindow: Falling tide moving towards low water
area7.missionRole: Covered the mid-inner estuary before low tide, using the second pair of flights on Day 1.
area7.operationalNote: This section sat in a stronger low-tide window than Area 8 and gave a cleaner pre-low-water view of the inland channel system.
area7.weatherNotes: Day 1, 18 April 2026. Midday survey window on the falling tide. Low tide reference for Wadebridge / upper estuary was about 12:56 BST.
area7.surveyNotes: This was the second area group flown on 18 April, using flights three and four of the day. It captures the estuary as it moved closer to low water and should be a stronger comparison window than the earlier Wadebridge block.
area7.purpose: Shows the Slate Quarry reach as the tide dropped closer to low water, giving a more exposed inland comparison than the earlier Area 8 pass.

area4.tideWindow: Crossing low water into the early return
area4.missionRole: Captured the Padstow Harbour Estuary in the best Day 1 low-tide window, using the middle three flights of the session.
area4.operationalNote: This was the key Area 4 repeat block and is the strongest first-day candidate for change analysis because it straddles low water directly.
area4.weatherNotes: Day 1, 18 April 2026. Main low-tide capture window. Low tide reference for Wadebridge / upper estuary was about 12:56 BST, so this block begins just before low water and continues into the first part of the flood.
area4.surveyNotes: This area used flights five, six, and seven of the day and sits closest to low tide. It is the main Day 1 repeat-survey block for testing section change and sandbar change outputs because the estuary bed was at or near its most exposed state.
area4.purpose: Shows the Padstow Harbour estuary around low tide, making it the clearest Day 1 repeat-survey window for exposed sandbar and surface-change interpretation.

area6.missionRole: Closed Day 1 with the final two flights, covering the inland Area 6 block after the tide had already turned.
area6.operationalNote: This section was surveyed later in the day once the estuary was beginning to refill, so it should be read as a post-low-tide repeat block rather than a maximum-exposure window.
area6.weatherNotes: Day 1, 18 April 2026. Later afternoon survey window after low tide. Low tide reference for Wadebridge / upper estuary was about 12:56 BST.
area6.surveyNotes: This was the last area group flown on 18 April, using the final two flights of the day. By this stage the tide had already turned and was beginning to return, so the area should be interpreted as a later-window repeat rather than a low-water maximum.
area6.purpose: Shows Old Town Cove after low tide, capturing the area once the water had started to return rather than at the maximum exposure point.

area1.missionRole: Opened Day 2 with the first two flights, covering the outer-estuary Area 1 block before low tide.
area1.operationalNote: This was the first lower-estuary block of the day and gives a clear pre-low-tide view before the session moved through the remaining outer and mid-estuary areas.
area1.weatherNotes: Day 2, 19 April 2026. Late-morning survey window on the falling tide. Estuary low tide reference was about 13:35 BST.
area1.surveyNotes: This area used the first two flights on 19 April. It captures the outer Padstow-side block before low water, with plenty of exposed ground but still some water present in the lower channels.
area1.purpose: Shows Hawker's Cove / Tregirls Beach on the falling tide before low water, giving the first Day 2 repeat view of the lower estuary.

area3.tideWindow: Crossing low water into the early return
area3.missionRole: Captured Area 3 in the strongest Day 2 low-tide window, using the second pair of flights.
area3.operationalNote: This block straddled low water and should be one of the clearest Day 2 survey windows for exposed bar and shoreline comparison.
area3.weatherNotes: Day 2, 19 April 2026. Main low-tide capture window. Estuary low tide reference was about 13:35 BST, so this block starts before low water and ends soon after the turn.
area3.surveyNotes: This area used flights three and four of the day. It sits closest to low tide on Day 2 and should offer one of the best repeat-survey comparisons for the lower estuary.
area3.purpose: Shows Rock Beach around low tide, making it the clearest Day 2 repeat-survey window for exposed ground and change interpretation.

area5.missionRole: Covered Area 5 after low tide, using the middle-late pair of flights on Day 2.
area5.operationalNote: This area was surveyed once the tide had already turned, so it should be read as an early-flood repeat block rather than a maximum-exposure moment.
area5.weatherNotes: Day 2, 19 April 2026. Early afternoon survey window after low tide. Estuary low tide reference was about 13:35 BST.
area5.surveyNotes: This area used flights five and six of the day. By this point the tide was returning, so the area offers a useful repeat comparison but not the most exposed state of the intertidal surface.
area5.purpose: Shows Porthilly Cove after low tide, capturing the area once water had started to return across the central transition zone.

area2.tideWindow: Later flood tide well after low water
area2.missionRole: Closed Day 2 with the final two flights, returning to Area 2 well after low tide.
area2.operationalNote: This was the latest lower-estuary block of the day and should be read as a later-tide repeat window rather than a low-water maximum.
area2.weatherNotes: Day 2, 19 April 2026. Later afternoon survey window with the tide well into its return. Estuary low tide reference was about 13:35 BST.
area2.surveyNotes: This area used the last two flights on 19 April. It gives a later-tide repeat view of the outer estuary once water had already returned to the lower channels and margins.
area2.purpose: Shows Daymer Bay later in the tide cycle, giving a repeat-survey view of the outer entrance once the estuary had started to refill.

2026-04-18.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside one fixed sandbar outline
2026-04-18.cellSize: Survey grid size approx 0.0651 m x 0.0651 m (cell area approx 0.00423778 m2)

area4.method: Later survey ground surface compared with the earlier survey inside the fixed Area 4 sandbar outline
area4.cellSize: Survey grid size approx 0.0651 m x 0.0651 m (cell area approx 0.00423778 m2)
area4.notes: Single monitored sandbar in Area 4. This first pass adds up where material appears to have built up or been lost inside the same fixed outline.
area4.previewCaption: These first preview maps place the measured change overlay on both survey contexts so people can see where the monitored sandbar appears to be building up or wearing away inside the measured zone.

polygons.label: Harbour Sandbar
polygons.summary: Strong net accretion between 22-23 Mar 2026 and 18-19 Apr 2026, with smaller local losses.

workflow.title: Add the survey round once
workflow.copy: Create one survey entry with its date, status, and notes instead of cloning multiple apps.

workflow.title: Attach outputs by area
workflow.copy: For each area, point the system at the new ortho, DSM, contour, and section outputs generated for that survey round.

workflow.title: Keep the interface stable
workflow.copy: The viewer, section panel, and project context all reuse the same UI, so only the data changes.

workflow.title: Add comparison later
workflow.copy: Once repeat-survey imagery is aligned per area, this same system can add true before-and-after viewers and change metrics without restructuring again.
